concepts.title: Position of India in the world map
concepts.description: India is located in South Asia, between 8°4' and 37°6' north latitude and 68°7' and 97°25' east longitude.

concepts.title: India's boundary with Pakistan
concepts.description: India shares a 3,190 km long international border with Pakistan, which is the world's highest militarized border.

concepts.title: India's coastline
concepts.description: India has a 7,517 km long coastline along the Arabian Sea, the Bay of Bengal, and the Indian Ocean.

concepts.title: Identifying the geographical location of India
concepts.description: Mnemonic: 'South Asia's South-West' - Helps in remembering that India is located in the south-west part of the Asian continent.

concepts.title: Confusion between the Himalayas and the Vindhyas
concepts.description: Students often get confused between the Himalayas (the world's highest mountain range) and the Vindhyas (a hill system in central India). Key point: The Himalayas are located in the north, while the Vindhyas are located in the centre of India.

concepts.title: India's geographical coordinates
concepts.description: Latitude: 8°4' to 37°6' N, Longitude: 68°7' to 97°25' E.

concepts.title: India's geographical position and its effects
concepts.description: India's geographical position between the tropics and the Himalayas shields the country from extreme climatic conditions and facilitates trade and cultural exchange.

concepts.title: India's position in the Asian continent
concepts.description: India is an integral part of the Asian continent and is bounded by the Himalayas in the north, the Arabian Sea in the west, the Bay of Bengal in the east, and the Indian Ocean in the south.
